# react-unique-id-generator - Full API Reference

> Unique IDs for React. Readable. Predictable. SSR-safe.

Version: 2.2.0
License: MIT
Peer dependency: react >=16.8.0
Zero runtime dependencies.
Bundle: ESM + CJS, tree-shakeable (sideEffects: false)

## Install

npm install react-unique-id-generator

## Imports

```tsx
import {
  IdProvider,
  useUniqueId,
  useUniqueIds,
  useIdContext,
  createServerIdManager,
  generateAutomationId,
  useAutomationId,
  AutomationIdPool,
  resetAutomationCounter,

  // v2.1 additions
  generateSecureId,
  nextIdForScope,
  resetIdForScope,
  resetAllScopes,
  getScopeCounter,
  getActiveScopes,
  useTrackedUniqueId,
  useTrackedUniqueIds,
  useIdMetrics,
  getIdMetrics,
  resetIdMetrics,
  getActiveIdCount,
  cleanupInactiveIds,
  OVERFLOW_THRESHOLD,

  // v2.2 additions
  generateIdWithStrategy,
  useIdWithStrategy,
  numericStrategy,
  zeroPaddedStrategy,
  timestampStrategy,
  hashStrategy,
  resetStrategyCounter,
  getStrategyCounter,
  CollisionDetector,
  checkCollision,
  getGlobalCollisionDetector,
  resetGlobalCollisionDetector,
  IdPool,
  useIdPool,
  SSRProvider,
  useSSRSafeId,
  useSSRContext,
  createSSRIdFactory,
  generateDelimitedId,
  useDelimitedId,
  resetDelimiterCounter,
  getDelimiterCounter,
} from 'react-unique-id-generator';

// Legacy (deprecated, still functional):
import nextId, {
  resetId,
  setGlobalPrefix,
  setGlobalSuffix,
  getCurrentId,
  setId,
  generateId,
} from 'react-unique-id-generator';
```

## TypeScript Types

```tsx
import type {
  IdProviderProps,
  IdContextValue,
  ServerIdManager,
  ServerIdManagerOptions,
  AutomationIdStrategy,   // 'sequential' | 'kebab-case' | 'camelCase' | 'custom'
  AutomationIdOptions,
  IdMetrics,              // { totalGenerated, activeCount, peakActiveCount }

  // v2.2 additions
  IdStrategy,             // { generate(prefix, suffix, counter): string }
  CollisionAction,        // 'warn' | 'throw' | 'skip'
  CollisionDetectorOptions,
  IdPoolOptions,
  SSRContextValue,
  SSRProviderProps,
  DelimitedIdOptions,
} from 'react-unique-id-generator';
```

---

## Provider API

### `<IdProvider>`

React context provider that scopes ID state (counter, prefix, suffix) to its subtree.
Each provider maintains an independent counter. Supports nesting and parallel providers.

Props:
- `prefix?: string` (default: `""`) - Prefix applied to all IDs within the provider
- `suffix?: string` (default: `""`) - Suffix appended to all IDs within the provider
- `startId?: number` (default: `0`) - Initial counter value (first ID = startId + 1)
- `children: ReactNode`

```tsx
// Basic usage
<IdProvider prefix="app-" suffix="-id">
  <YourComponent />
</IdProvider>

// Nested providers (independent scopes)
<IdProvider prefix="page-">
  <Header />          {/* useUniqueId() => "page-1" */}
  <IdProvider prefix="modal-">
    <ModalContent />  {/* useUniqueId() => "modal-1" */}
  </IdProvider>
  <Footer />          {/* useUniqueId() => "page-2" */}
</IdProvider>

// Parallel providers (fully isolated)
<IdProvider prefix="sidebar-">
  <NavItem />   {/* "sidebar-1" */}
</IdProvider>
<IdProvider prefix="main-">
  <Card />      {/* "main-1" */}
</IdProvider>
```

### `useIdContext(): IdContextValue | null`

Access the current provider's context value. Returns `null` when called outside a provider.

```tsx
const ctx = useIdContext();
// ctx.prefix, ctx.suffix, ctx.nextId(localPrefix?)
```

---

## Hooks

### `useUniqueId(localPrefix?: string): string`

Generates a stable unique ID for a component instance. Created once on mount, stable across re-renders.
When inside an `<IdProvider>`, scoped to that provider. Outside a provider, falls back to global state.

The `localPrefix` argument overrides the provider's prefix for that call.

```tsx
function MyComponent() {
  const id = useUniqueId('my-input-');  // "my-input-1"
  return <input id={id} />;
}
```

### `useUniqueIds(count: number, localPrefix?: string): string[]`

Generates multiple stable unique IDs at once.

```tsx
function LoginForm() {
  const [emailId, passwordId] = useUniqueIds(2, 'login-');
  return (
    <form>
      <label htmlFor={emailId}>Email</label>
      <input id={emailId} type="email" />
      <label htmlFor={passwordId}>Password</label>
      <input id={passwordId} type="password" />
    </form>
  );
}
```

---

## Server-Side Rendering

### `createServerIdManager(options?): ServerIdManager`

Creates an isolated ID manager for SSR. Each request should create its own manager.

Options:
- `prefix?: string` (default: `""`)
- `suffix?: string` (default: `""`)
- `startId?: number` (default: `0`)

ServerIdManager methods:
- `nextId(localPrefix?: string): string` - Generate the next ID
- `resetId(): void` - Reset counter to 0
- `getCurrentId(): number` - Get current counter value
- `setId(id: number): void` - Set counter to specific value

```ts
// Express middleware example
app.get('*', (req, res) => {
  const ids = createServerIdManager({ prefix: 'ssr-' });
  const id1 = ids.nextId();         // "ssr-1"
  const id2 = ids.nextId();         // "ssr-2"
  const id3 = ids.nextId('nav-');   // "nav-3"
  // Render your app...
});

// Next.js server action
export async function generatePageIds() {
  const ids = createServerIdManager({ prefix: 'page-', suffix: '-el' });
  return {
    heroId: ids.nextId(),      // "page-1-el"
    contentId: ids.nextId(),   // "page-2-el"
  };
}
```

---

## Automation Testing

### `generateAutomationId(componentName: string, options?: AutomationIdOptions): string`

Generate a single automation ID with a naming strategy.

Options:
- `strategy?: 'sequential' | 'kebab-case' | 'camelCase' | 'custom'` (default: `'sequential'`)
- `prefix?: string` (default: `""`)
- `separator?: string` (default: `"-"`)
- `customFn?: (name: string, index: number) => string` (required for `'custom'` strategy)

```ts
generateAutomationId('Button')                                    // "Button-1"
generateAutomationId('UserProfile', { strategy: 'kebab-case' })   // "user-profile-2"
generateAutomationId('myButton', { strategy: 'camelCase' })       // "myButton-3"
generateAutomationId('Btn', { prefix: 'qa-', separator: '_' })   // "qa-Btn_4"

// Custom strategy
generateAutomationId('LoginForm', {
  strategy: 'custom',
  customFn: (name, index) => `e2e__${name}__${index}`,
});
// => "e2e__LoginForm__5"
```

### `useAutomationId(componentName: string, options?: AutomationIdOptions): string`

React hook version. Returns a stable automation ID that persists across re-renders.

```tsx
function SubmitButton() {
  const testId = useAutomationId('SubmitButton', { strategy: 'kebab-case' });
  return <button data-testid={testId}>Submit</button>;
  // => <button data-testid="submit-button-1">Submit</button>
}
```

### `AutomationIdPool`

Pre-allocates a pool of automation IDs for performance in large applications.

```ts
const pool = new AutomationIdPool(100, 'component', {
  strategy: 'kebab-case',
  prefix: 'test-',
});

const id1 = pool.acquire();   // "test-component-1"
const id2 = pool.acquire();   // "test-component-2"
pool.release(id1);             // Return to pool
const id3 = pool.acquire();   // "test-component-1" (reused)
pool.drain();                  // Clear all
pool.size;                     // Number of available IDs
```

### `resetAutomationCounter(): void`

Resets the global automation counter to 0. Useful in test setup.

```ts
beforeEach(() => {
  resetAutomationCounter();
});
```

---

## Secure ID Generation

### `generateSecureId(length?: number): string`

Generates a cryptographically random ID string. Uses the best available source:
1. `crypto.randomUUID()` — returns a UUID v4 string (ignores `length` param)
2. `crypto.getRandomValues()` — returns a hex string of the specified length
3. `Math.random()` fallback — returns a hex string (not cryptographically secure)

Parameters:
- `length?: number` (default: `32`) - Length of hex string (only used when `randomUUID` is unavailable)

```ts
generateSecureId()      // "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890" (UUID)
generateSecureId(16)    // "a1b2c3d4e5f67890" (16-char hex, when randomUUID unavailable)
generateSecureId(8)     // "f3a1b2c4" (8-char hex)
```

Use cases: CSRF tokens, session identifiers, nonces, unique keys where sequential IDs are inappropriate.

---

## Scoped ID Management

Independent counters per named scope. Reset one module without affecting others.

### `nextIdForScope(scope: string, prefix?: string): string`

Generate the next sequential ID within a named scope.

```ts
nextIdForScope('form', 'field-')   // "field-1"
nextIdForScope('modal', 'modal-')  // "modal-1"
nextIdForScope('form', 'field-')   // "field-2" (form scope continues)
```

### `resetIdForScope(scope: string): void`

Reset the counter for a specific scope to 0. Does not affect other scopes.

```ts
nextIdForScope('form')    // "1"
nextIdForScope('form')    // "2"
resetIdForScope('form')
nextIdForScope('form')    // "1" (reset)
```

### `resetAllScopes(): void`

Reset all scope counters at once.

### `getScopeCounter(scope: string): number`

Get the current counter value for a scope. Returns `0` for unused scopes.

### `getActiveScopes(): string[]`

Returns an array of all scope names that have been used and not reset.

```ts
nextIdForScope('form')
nextIdForScope('modal')
getActiveScopes()  // ["form", "modal"]

resetIdForScope('form')
getActiveScopes()  // ["modal"]
```

---

## Performance Monitoring & Tracked IDs

### `useTrackedUniqueId(localPrefix?: string): string`

Like `useUniqueId`, but registers the ID in an internal tracking registry and automatically removes it when the component unmounts. Ideal for dynamic lists where components frequently mount and unmount.

```tsx
function ListItem({ text }) {
  const id = useTrackedUniqueId('item-');  // tracked, auto-cleaned on unmount
  return <li id={id}>{text}</li>;
}
```

### `useTrackedUniqueIds(count: number, localPrefix?: string): string[]`

Like `useUniqueIds`, but all generated IDs are tracked and cleaned up on unmount.

```tsx
const [titleId, descId] = useTrackedUniqueIds(2, 'dialog-');
// Both removed from registry when component unmounts
```

### `useIdMetrics(): IdMetrics`

React hook that subscribes to real-time ID metrics. Re-renders the component when metrics change. Uses `useSyncExternalStore` internally.

Returns:
- `totalGenerated: number` - Total tracked IDs ever created (resets with `resetIdMetrics()`)
- `activeCount: number` - Currently mounted tracked IDs
- `peakActiveCount: number` - Highest simultaneous active ID count

```tsx
function DebugPanel() {
  const metrics = useIdMetrics();
  return (
    <pre>
      Active: {metrics.activeCount}
      Peak:   {metrics.peakActiveCount}
      Total:  {metrics.totalGenerated}
    </pre>
  );
}
```

### `getIdMetrics(): IdMetrics`

Non-hook version. Returns the current snapshot of metrics.

### `getActiveIdCount(): number`

Returns the number of currently tracked (mounted) IDs.

### `resetIdMetrics(): void`

Resets `totalGenerated` to 0. Does not affect active tracking.

### `cleanupInactiveIds(): void`

Clears the entire tracking registry. Useful in test teardown.

---

## Overflow Protection

All counters (global `nextId`, `IdProvider`, `createServerIdManager`) emit a one-time `console.warn` in development when they exceed `OVERFLOW_THRESHOLD` (1,000,000 IDs). The warning resets when the counter is reset.

```ts
import { OVERFLOW_THRESHOLD } from 'react-unique-id-generator';
// OVERFLOW_THRESHOLD === 1_000_000

// In dev mode, when any counter crosses 1M:
// ⚠️ [react-unique-id-generator] ID counter has exceeded 1000000.
//    This may indicate a memory leak or unintended usage pattern.
```

This helps catch:
- Components that generate IDs on every render instead of memoizing
- Missing cleanup in rapidly mounting/unmounting component trees
- Runaway loops that generate IDs unintentionally

---

## Custom ID Strategies

### `IdStrategy` interface

```ts
interface IdStrategy {
  generate(prefix: string, suffix: string, counter: number): string;
}
```

### Built-in strategies

- `numericStrategy` - Default format: `"prefix1suffix"`
- `zeroPaddedStrategy(width?)` - Zero-padded counter: `"prefix0001suffix"` (default width: 4)
- `timestampStrategy` - Timestamp + counter: `"prefix1714300000000-1suffix"`
- `hashStrategy` - Deterministic 8-char hex hash: `"prefixa3f2b1c0suffix"`

### `generateIdWithStrategy(strategy: IdStrategy, prefix?: string, suffix?: string): string`

Generate an ID using a strategy outside of React components. Increments an internal strategy counter.

```ts
generateIdWithStrategy(numericStrategy, 'item-')        // "item-1"
generateIdWithStrategy(zeroPaddedStrategy(4), 'id-')    // "id-0002"
generateIdWithStrategy(hashStrategy, 'h-')              // "h-a3f2b1c0"
```

### `useIdWithStrategy(strategy: IdStrategy, prefix?: string, suffix?: string): string`

React hook that generates a stable ID using the given strategy. Persists across re-renders.
Works with `<IdProvider>` context when available.

```tsx
function OrderItem() {
  const id = useIdWithStrategy(zeroPaddedStrategy(6), 'order-');
  return <div id={id}>...</div>;
  // => <div id="order-000001">...</div>
}
```

### Custom strategy example

```ts
const base36Strategy: IdStrategy = {
  generate(prefix, suffix, counter) {
    return `${prefix}${counter.toString(36)}${suffix}`;
  },
};

generateIdWithStrategy(base36Strategy, 'x-')  // "x-1", "x-2", ... "x-a"
```

### `resetStrategyCounter(): void`

Reset the strategy counter to 0. Useful in test setup.

### `getStrategyCounter(): number`

Get the current strategy counter value.

---

## Collision Detection

Track generated IDs and detect duplicates. Useful for debugging, testing, or enforcing uniqueness.

### `CollisionDetector`

```ts
const detector = new CollisionDetector({ action: 'throw' });
```

Constructor options:
- `action?: 'warn' | 'throw' | 'skip'` (default: `'warn'`) - Behavior on collision
- `maxSize?: number` (default: `100000`) - Maximum registry size before warning

Methods:
- `register(id: string): boolean` - Register an ID. Returns `false` on collision
- `has(id: string): boolean` - Check if an ID is registered
- `unregister(id: string): boolean` - Remove an ID from the registry
- `clear(): void` - Clear all registrations and reset collision count
- `getRegisteredIds(): string[]` - Get all registered IDs
- `size: number` - Number of registered IDs (getter)
- `collisions: number` - Total collisions detected (getter)

```ts
const detector = new CollisionDetector({ action: 'throw' });
detector.register('input-1');   // true
detector.register('input-2');   // true
detector.register('input-1');   // throws Error: ID collision detected
detector.has('input-1');        // true
detector.unregister('input-1'); // true
detector.clear();               // reset everything
```

### `checkCollision(id: string, action?: CollisionAction): boolean`

Convenience function using a global singleton detector.
Returns `true` if ID was registered successfully, `false` on collision.

```ts
checkCollision('field-1');          // true
checkCollision('field-1', 'skip');  // false (duplicate)
checkCollision('field-1', 'throw'); // throws Error
```

### `getGlobalCollisionDetector(options?): CollisionDetector`

Access (or create) the global singleton collision detector.

### `resetGlobalCollisionDetector(): void`

Reset the global detector (clears registry, destroys singleton).

---

## ID Pool Management

Pre-allocate IDs for high-throughput scenarios. The `IdPool` class is a generic pool
(distinct from `AutomationIdPool` which is automation-specific).

### `IdPool`

```ts
const pool = new IdPool(size: number, options?: IdPoolOptions)
```

Options:
- `prefix?: string` (default: `""`) - Prefix for generated IDs
- `suffix?: string` (default: `""`) - Suffix for generated IDs
- `autoRefill?: boolean` (default: `true`) - Auto-generate new IDs when pool is exhausted
- `generator?: (index: number) => string` - Custom ID generator function

Methods:
- `acquire(): string` - Get the next available ID (reuses released IDs first)
- `release(id: string): void` - Return an ID to the pool for reuse
- `drain(): void` - Clear all available and released IDs
- `refill(count: number): void` - Add more pre-generated IDs to the pool
- `size: number` - Number of available + released IDs (getter)
- `totalGenerated: number` - Total IDs ever generated by this pool (getter)

```ts
const pool = new IdPool(100, { prefix: 'widget-', suffix: '-el' });
const id1 = pool.acquire();  // "widget-1-el"
const id2 = pool.acquire();  // "widget-2-el"
pool.release(id1);           // Return to pool
pool.acquire();              // "widget-1-el" (reused)
pool.drain();                // Clear all
pool.refill(50);             // Add 50 new IDs

// Custom generator
const custom = new IdPool(50, {
  generator: (i) => `session-${Date.now()}-${i}`,
});
```

### `useIdPool(size: number, prefix?: string): () => string`

React hook that pre-generates a pool of IDs and returns an `acquire` function.
The pool is stable across re-renders. Works with `<IdProvider>` context when available.

```tsx
function DynamicList({ items }) {
  const acquireId = useIdPool(20, 'item-');
  return items.map((item) => (
    <li key={acquireId()} id={acquireId()}>
      {item.name}
    </li>
  ));
}
```

---

## SSR Utilities (v2.2)

Enhanced server-side rendering support beyond `createServerIdManager`.
These provide React context-based isolation and request-scoped ID namespacing.

### `<SSRProvider>`

React context provider that scopes IDs per server request. Use `requestId` to namespace
IDs for deterministic, collision-free SSR output.

Props:
- `prefix?: string` (default: `""`) - Default prefix for IDs
- `suffix?: string` (default: `""`) - Suffix appended to all IDs
- `requestId?: string` - Request identifier for namespacing (e.g., a short UUID)
- `children: ReactNode`

```tsx
// Server render
function App({ requestId }) {
  return (
    <SSRProvider requestId={requestId} prefix="app-" suffix="-el">
      <Form />
    </SSRProvider>
  );
}

function Form() {
  const emailId = useSSRSafeId('email-');  // "email-req123-1-el"
  const passId = useSSRSafeId('pass-');    // "pass-req123-2-el"
  return (
    <form>
      <label htmlFor={emailId}>Email</label>
      <input id={emailId} />
    </form>
  );
}
```

### `useSSRSafeId(prefix?: string): string`

React hook for generating IDs inside an `<SSRProvider>`. Falls back to a timestamp-based
ID outside a provider (with a dev warning). Stable across re-renders.

### `useSSRContext(): SSRContextValue | null`

Access the SSR provider context directly. Returns `null` outside an `<SSRProvider>`.

SSRContextValue methods:
- `nextId(prefix?: string): string` - Generate next request-scoped ID
- `resetId(): void` - Reset counter to 0
- `getCurrentId(): number` - Get current counter value

### `createSSRIdFactory(requestId: string, prefix?: string, suffix?: string)`

Non-React factory for server-side ID generation with request scoping.
Each factory is fully independent.

Returns:
- `nextId(localPrefix?: string): string` - Generate next request-scoped ID
- `resetId(): void` - Reset counter to 0
- `getCurrentId(): number` - Get current counter value

```ts
// Express middleware
app.get('*', (req, res) => {
  const ids = createSSRIdFactory(req.id, 'ssr-');
  const headerId = ids.nextId();     // "ssr-abc123-1"
  const contentId = ids.nextId();    // "ssr-abc123-2"
  ids.resetId();                     // Reset for reuse
});
```

---

## Custom Delimiters

Control how prefix, counter, and suffix are joined. By default, the library concatenates
parts directly (`prefix + counter + suffix`). The delimiter API lets you specify any separator.

### `generateDelimitedId(options?: DelimitedIdOptions): string`

Generate an ID with parts joined by a custom delimiter. Empty parts are omitted.

Options:
- `prefix?: string` (default: `""`) - Prefix part
- `suffix?: string` (default: `""`) - Suffix part
- `delimiter?: string` (default: `"-"`) - Separator between parts

```ts
generateDelimitedId({ prefix: 'app', delimiter: '.' })
// "app.1"

generateDelimitedId({ prefix: 'com', suffix: 'widget', delimiter: '.' })
// "com.2.widget"

generateDelimitedId({ prefix: 'ns', suffix: 'item', delimiter: '::' })
// "ns::3::item"

generateDelimitedId()  // "1" (no prefix/suffix, just counter)
```

### `useDelimitedId(prefix?: string, delimiter?: string, suffix?: string): string`

React hook version. Returns a stable delimited ID across re-renders.
Works with `<IdProvider>` context when available.

```tsx
function Widget() {
  const id = useDelimitedId('widget', '.', 'container');
  return <div id={id}>...</div>;
  // => <div id="widget.1.container">...</div>
}
```

### `resetDelimiterCounter(): void`

Reset the delimiter counter to 0.

### `getDelimiterCounter(): number`

Get the current delimiter counter value.

---

## Legacy Functions (Deprecated)

These use global state. They emit a one-time deprecation warning in development.
Use `<IdProvider>` and hooks instead for SSR safety.

### `nextId(localPrefix?: string | null): string`

```ts
nextId()           // "1"
nextId('input-')   // "input-2"
```

### `generateId(prefix?: string, suffix?: string): string`

```ts
generateId('btn-', '-primary') // "btn-1-primary"
```

### `setGlobalPrefix(prefix: string): void`

Use `<IdProvider prefix="...">` instead.

### `setGlobalSuffix(suffix: string): void`

Use `<IdProvider suffix="...">` instead.

### `resetId(): void`

Reset counter to 0.

### `getCurrentId(): number`

Get current counter value.

### `setId(id: number): void`

Set counter to specific value. Negatives clamped to 0, decimals floored.

---

## Common Patterns

### Accessible form fields (label + input linking)

```tsx
<IdProvider>
  <TextField label="Email" type="email" />
  <TextField label="Password" type="password" />
</IdProvider>

function TextField({ label, type }) {
  const id = useUniqueId('field-');
  return (
    <div>
      <label htmlFor={id}>{label}</label>
      <input id={id} type={type} />
    </div>
  );
}
```

### ARIA attributes (dialog labeling)

```tsx
function Modal({ title, children }) {
  const [titleId, descId] = useUniqueIds(2, 'modal-');
  return (
    <div role="dialog" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={descId}>
      <h2 id={titleId}>{title}</h2>
      <p id={descId}>{children}</p>
    </div>
  );
}
```

### Next.js App Router (root layout)

```tsx
// app/layout.tsx
import { IdProvider } from 'react-unique-id-generator';

export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
  return (
    <html><body>
      <IdProvider prefix="app-">{children}</IdProvider>
    </body></html>
  );
}
```

### E2E test IDs with Playwright/Cypress

```tsx
function LoginButton() {
  const testId = useAutomationId('LoginButton', { strategy: 'kebab-case' });
  return <button data-testid={testId}>Log in</button>;
  // data-testid="login-button-1"
}

// In Playwright:
// await page.getByTestId('login-button-1').click();
```

### Secure tokens for forms

```tsx
import { generateSecureId } from 'react-unique-id-generator';

function CSRFToken() {
  const token = generateSecureId();
  return <input type="hidden" name="_csrf" value={token} />;
}
```

### Scoped ID management across modules

```ts
import { nextIdForScope, resetIdForScope } from 'react-unique-id-generator';

// Checkout module
nextIdForScope('checkout', 'cart-');  // "cart-1"
nextIdForScope('checkout', 'cart-');  // "cart-2"

// Profile module (independent counter)
nextIdForScope('profile', 'usr-');   // "usr-1"

// Reset checkout only
resetIdForScope('checkout');
nextIdForScope('checkout', 'cart-');  // "cart-1" (reset)
nextIdForScope('profile', 'usr-');   // "usr-2" (unaffected)
```

### Memory-efficient dynamic lists

```tsx
import { useTrackedUniqueId, getActiveIdCount } from 'react-unique-id-generator';

function TodoItem({ text }) {
  const id = useTrackedUniqueId('todo-');
  return <li id={id}>{text}</li>;
  // ID auto-removed from registry when this component unmounts
}

// Monitor active IDs in dev
console.log(getActiveIdCount()); // matches number of mounted TodoItems
```

### Dev-only performance dashboard

```tsx
import { useIdMetrics } from 'react-unique-id-generator';

function IdDebugger() {
  const { activeCount, peakActiveCount, totalGenerated } = useIdMetrics();
  return (
    <div className="debug-bar">
      Active: {activeCount} | Peak: {peakActiveCount} | Total: {totalGenerated}
    </div>
  );
}
```

### Custom BEM-style strategy

```tsx
import { useIdWithStrategy } from 'react-unique-id-generator';
import type { IdStrategy } from 'react-unique-id-generator';

const bemStrategy: IdStrategy = {
  generate(prefix, suffix, counter) {
    return `${prefix}__element-${counter}${suffix ? `--${suffix}` : ''}`;
  },
};

function Card() {
  const id = useIdWithStrategy(bemStrategy, 'card', 'active');
  return <div id={id}>...</div>;
  // => <div id="card__element-1--active">...</div>
}
```

### Collision-safe ID generation

```ts
import { CollisionDetector, generateIdWithStrategy, numericStrategy } from 'react-unique-id-generator';

const detector = new CollisionDetector({ action: 'throw' });

function safeGenerateId(prefix: string): string {
  const id = generateIdWithStrategy(numericStrategy, prefix);
  detector.register(id);  // Throws if duplicate
  return id;
}
```

### Dynamic list with pre-allocated ID pool

```tsx
import { useIdPool } from 'react-unique-id-generator';

function TodoList({ items }) {
  const acquireId = useIdPool(items.length, 'todo-');
  return (
    <ul>
      {items.map((item) => {
        const id = acquireId();
        return (
          <li key={id}>
            <label htmlFor={id}>{item}</label>
            <input id={id} type="checkbox" />
          </li>
        );
      })}
    </ul>
  );
}
```

### SSR with request-scoped IDs

```tsx
import { SSRProvider, useSSRSafeId } from 'react-unique-id-generator';

function handleRequest(requestId: string) {
  return renderToString(
    <SSRProvider requestId={requestId} prefix="app-">
      <Page />
    </SSRProvider>
  );
  // IDs: "app-abc123-1", "app-abc123-2", ...
}

function Page() {
  const headerId = useSSRSafeId('header-');
  const contentId = useSSRSafeId('content-');
  return (
    <div>
      <header id={headerId}>...</header>
      <main id={contentId}>...</main>
    </div>
  );
}
```

### Dot-delimited namespace IDs

```tsx
import { useDelimitedId } from 'react-unique-id-generator';

function Widget({ section }) {
  const id = useDelimitedId(section, '.', 'widget');
  return <div id={id}>...</div>;
  // useDelimitedId('sidebar', '.', 'widget') => "sidebar.1.widget"
}
```

---

## Framework Compatibility

- Next.js (App Router & Pages Router): Use <IdProvider> in root layout
- Vite + React: Works out of the box (ESM)
- Create React App: Works out of the box
- Remix: Use <IdProvider> in root route
- React 16.8 through React 19: Supported
